findings.description: hits.pop(0) is O(n) and is called in a while-loop on the per-request hot path (check()), making prefix pruning O(n^2) worst-case (e.g. an idle client whose whole window expired); use collections.deque so popleft()/hits[0]/append are all O(1).
The per-client hit log is a list, forcing O(n) pop(0) pruning; switching the backing structure to collections.deque is the root fix that turns the line-42 prune from O(n^2) into O(k).
current_usage does a full O(n) linear scan to count live timestamps even though the bucket is sorted (monotonic time.time() inserts); bisect.bisect_left on cutoff gives the same count in O(log n).

sweep() runs any(t >= cutoff for t in hits) over every bucket, which is O(total stored timestamps) and short-circuits the wrong way (scans oldest-first, so still-active buckets walk past all stale entries); checking hits[-1] >= cutoff makes the keep-decision O(1) and sweep O(num_buckets).
len(hits) <= self.limit followed by append admits limit+1 requests before rejecting (off-by-one over-admission) — flagged for critic-logic, not a performance issue.
